goal: Enterprise-oriented app monitoring list for Jamf-managed macOS devices and network telemetry keyed on dns.query, tls.sni, and http.hostname.

policy_tiers.name: Block Preferred
policy_tiers.description: Default-deny posture for high-confidence exfiltration channels or non-work apps. Use Jamf to remove/restrict native apps and network telemetry to alert or block web access.

policy_tiers.name: Managed Tenant Only
policy_tiers.description: Allow only sanctioned corporate tenants or managed instances. Alert on personal, guest, or unmanaged usage patterns.

policy_tiers.name: Monitor High Risk
policy_tiers.description: High-risk tools that may be approved for limited use. Inventory with Jamf where possible and correlate host presence with network signals.

category_defaults.reason: Agent frameworks can automate browsing, tool use, and file actions against external services.
Browser agents can access sensitive SaaS content and operate inside authenticated sessions.
Native assistants can bypass browser controls and are better governed through endpoint management.
Prompt and response tracing tools can retain sensitive model traffic and tool outputs.
Claw-family agents combine local execution, messaging control, and plugin or skill ecosystems that make unmanaged deployment especially risky.
Cloud drives should be limited to sanctioned tenants because persistent sync and share links are common leak paths.
Docs and knowledge bases may be legitimate, but unmanaged tenants create durable external exposure.
File conversion sites require document upload and should be limited to approved workflows only.
Ad-hoc transfer services provide direct external file movement with limited governance.
General LLM chat tools are common prompt and text exfiltration channels.
Coding assistants can upload repository context, diffs, and credentials to external providers.
Media generation tools can receive sensitive images, audio, or identity-related media.
Meeting bots and transcript services capture audio, summaries, and meeting artifacts externally.
Personal or unmanaged messaging channels move text, files, and screenshots outside enterprise retention controls.
P2P sync and torrent tools create uncontrolled transfer paths that evade tenant-based governance.
Paste sites are a direct code and secret publication path with low friction and high blast radius.
Remote control software can bypass endpoint controls and usually includes clipboard or file transfer features.
Screen recording tools create externally shared videos that frequently contain internal product and customer data.
Translation services often require full text or document submission and should remain approval-based.
